questions.description: While out walking his dog early one morning in a local field owned by his… Advise the parties as to who is entitled to…
questions.text: While out walking his dog early one morning in a local field owned by his neighbour Tom, Joe stumbled and slipped on a piece of earthy ground that had been disturbed by recent heavy rainfall. As he was brushing himself off, he noticed a shiny object protruding from the soil. Joe dusted off the object to discover it was an old antique watch which was engraved on the back "My dearest A, with all my love forever". Joe, thinking this would make a lovely gift for his father who was a dedicated antique collector, dropped the watch into his pocket. When he looked up, Joe saw that Alex (who sold Tom the land ten years ago) and Tom were both standing at the entrance to the field. Having witnessed what happened, they both claimed ownership of the watch. Advise the parties as to who is entitled to the find.

questions.description: Answer parts (a) [AND]{.underline} (b) (a) Bill and Ted were joint tenants… Advise the respective executors as to who…
questions.text: Answer parts (a) [AND]{.underline} (b) (a) Bill and Ted were joint tenants of a house in the Dublin suburbs. Last summer, they were travelling on a helicopter ride which crashed. Both were killed at the same instant when the pilot of the helicopter lost control. By his will, Ted left all his property to his friend, Jim. By his will, Bill left all his property to his two siblings, Anne and Barry. Advise the respective executors as to who is entitled to the house. [AND]{.underline} (b) Tom and Jerry studied dentistry together and after some time working in a large practice in Dublin, they decided to set up their own practice together. In 2009, they decided to purchase a property in Bray, Co Wicklow, to accommodate their practice. Each put 350,000 towards the purchase price of 700,000 and the property was conveyed "to Tom and Jerry". After Tom had an affair with Jerry's wife, relations between the two men became very strained. Jerry now wishes to set up his own practice in Dublin. He wants to force a sale of the property or to make Tom pay him a rent if Tom continues to occupy the premises. Exacerbated by all of the stress, Jerry has recently had a heart bypass operation and is also concerned as to whether his family would have a claim to the property if he happens to die before matters are resolved. Advise the parties.

The Succession Act 1965" ... is based on the idea that a testator owes a duty to leave part of his estate to his widow (the legal right) and to make proper provision for his children in accordance with his means. It is not based on a duty to provide maintenance for his widow, nor it is limited in its application to children who were dependent on him". Per Kenny J, FM v. TAM (1972) 106 IL TR 82, 86. Critically discuss this statement with reference to case law.

questions.description: Discuss the situations where a conveyance by a spouse/civil partner of an interest in the family/shared home will be…
questions.text: Discuss the situations where a conveyance by a spouse/civil partner of an interest in the family/shared home will be valid despite the absence of prior written consent by the other spouse/civil partner. Your answer should be supported by relevant legal authority.

questions.description: In 2006, Michael created a mortgage over his house in favour of his local Bank.
questions.text: In 2006, Michael created a mortgage over his house in favour of his local Bank. Due to losing his job almost eight months ago, he has now fallen behind in his mortgage repayments. The Bank now seeks your advice as to what the most appropriate methods are in order for it to enforce its security having regard to any potential challenges it may encounter along the way. Please support your answer with relevant legal authority.

questions.description: Is the Law of Adverse Possession in need of reform in your opinion?
questions.text: Is the Law of Adverse Possession in need of reform in your opinion? Support your answer with reference to relevant legal authority.

questions.description: In March 2013, Mel, who was a fit and healthy 28-year-old, noticed that… Advise as the terms and validity of the will.
questions.text: In March 2013, Mel, who was a fit and healthy 28-year-old, noticed that she had an unusual mark on her skin. Upon further investigation at the hospital, it was determined that Mel had skin melanoma (cancer), which needed to be removed as soon as possible and further treatment would be required. Before Mel went into hospital for treatment, her boyfriend, Mark, who was a solicitor, suggested that it may be a good idea to draw up a will since she had three-year old twins. Being a positive thinker and not really dwelling on it too much, Mel decided to draft a will for the sake of keeping Mark happy. She asked Mark to act as her solicitor by drawing up a preliminary will leaving all of her worldly items to her twin sons. Mark did so and dropped it by her house for signature the week before the operation was due to take place. However, a few days later, Mel entered a competition on the Weekend Show and won a car with €20,000 in the glovebox. She decided that it would be important to include this in her will also. When Mark called to the house the day before her operation to collect the signed will, Mel signed the original will document and added in handwriting, the car and money. She did this in the presence of Mark and her twin boys. Her neighbour Sheila was also walking into the room when Mel was initialling the will. "Sheila, you need to see this too! Mark called out to her. "I can see you signing it Mel" she replied as she had her back turned pouring tea. Later that evening, Sheila and Mel were alone sitting by the fire. The fire was nearly gone out so Sheila threw some old newspapers into the fire to try and keep it going. It was only after she threw in the newspaper that she noticed that the documents of the will were stuck in between the papers. She quickly removed the will from the fire, with the document being only partially burned. Mel's condition rapidly deteriorated after she stopped responding to treatment and she died a few weeks later. Advise as the terms and validity of the will.

questions.description: Discuss any [two]{.underline} off the following areas of land law: (a) Rights of residence (b) Estoppel licences (c)…
questions.text: Discuss any [two]{.underline} off the following areas of land law: (a) Rights of residence (b) Estoppel licences (c) The influence of equity in Irish Land Law

questions.description: The Land and Conveyancing Reform Act 2009 (as amended) aims to provide for the reform and modernisation of land law and…
questions.text: The Land and Conveyancing Reform Act 2009 (as amended) aims to provide for the reform and modernisation of land law and conveyancing in the Republic of Ireland. Based on your knowledge of Irish Conveyancing Law, has the 2009 Act succeeded in meeting tis aims to date?

questions.description: Molly successfully applied for a mortgage in 2008 so that she could build… Mortgage.
questions.text: Molly successfully applied for a mortgage in 2008 so that she could build an extension onto her café in a small village in Co. Mayo. Unfortunately, when the village was bypassed in 2017, Molly's business began to suffer to the extent that she felt she had to use her savings to cover the mortgage repayments in the absence of any profit being generated from the business. In January 2019, Molly started to fall into arrears on her mortgage repayments. Advise the bank what its options are in terms of enforcing its security of the loan to Molly. Refer to legal and policy developments in your answer.

questions.description: "In order to defeat the title of the original landowner, I am of the opinion that the adverse user must be of a…
questions.text: "In order to defeat the title of the original landowner, I am of the opinion that the adverse user must be of a definite and positive character and such as could leave no doubt in the mind of a landowner alerted to his rights that occupation adverse to his title was taking place. This is particularly the case when the parcel of land involved is for the first time being worthless or valueless for the purposes of the original owner." (O'Hanlon J, Doyle v. O'Neill (High Court, January 13th, 1995, unreported) Critically analyse whether or not this statement provided an accurate reflection of current Irish law on adverse possession. Please make references to relevant legal authority in your answer.

questions.description: Answer (a) [AND]{.underline} (b) (a) Rob and Janet were joint tenants of a… Advise the respective executors as to who…
questions.text: Answer (a) [AND]{.underline} (b) (a) Rob and Janet were joint tenants of a house in Galway city centre. Last year, they went on a helicopter ride together which unfortunately crashed due to adverse weather conditions. Both were killed instantly. In his will, Rob left all his property to his friend, Trish. By her will, Janet left all her property to her two siblings George and Fred. Advise the respective executors as to who is entitled to the house. [AND]{.underline} (b) John died unexpectedly from a short illness in December 2018. At the time of his death, he was living on the family farm (which he had inherited some 30 years previous) with his wife Julie. In his will, John left all of his worldly belongings to the cats and dogs' home. John has a daughter -- Mavis from a previous relationship, to whom he left nothing under his will. Upon discovering her father's death, Mavis claims she was left out of the will as she did not get along with her father. However, she does acknowledge that her father gave her €15,000 over 10 years ago for college expenses and living costs. Advise Mavis.

questions.description: The Residential Tenancies Act 2004 (as amended) contains a number of provisions for the private rented sector in…
questions.text: The Residential Tenancies Act 2004 (as amended) contains a number of provisions for the private rented sector in Ireland, in particular, in the area of security of tenure and tenancy termination procedures. Consider whether the Residential Tenancies Act 2004 has succeeded in its aim of providing a clear legislative framework for tenancy obligations for landlords and tenants in Ireland.

questions.description: In 1978, Tom built a house on a site, much of which was bog land, adjacent… Advise Tom.
questions.text: In 1978, Tom built a house on a site, much of which was bog land, adjacent to Jerry's land. The conservatory of the house overlooked Jerry's land which was a large field with some beautiful woods at the end of it. The only practical means of access to the nearby road is through Jerry's field, because the driveway which Tom built on his own land is almost always flooded. Tom has therefore continually used the field since 1980. This year, Jerry has sold the field to a well-known property developer whose plans for development include the erection of a ten-foot boundary wall. The wall will cut off access to the road from the field and it will considerably reduce the amount of light received through the conservatory and destroy Tom's view of the woods. Advise Tom.

questions.description: Answer (a) [AND]{.underline} (b) (a) Critically analyse the rules and principles which govern the construction of will…
questions.text: Answer (a) [AND]{.underline} (b) (a) Critically analyse the rules and principles which govern the construction of will under Irish Succession Law. [AND]{.underline} (b) Briefly discuss the rules and principles which dictate how an intestate's estate should be distributed.

questions.description: Frank and Harry were in business together as solicitors and, in 2012,… Advise Harry.
questions.text: Frank and Harry were in business together as solicitors and, in 2012, decided to purchase a property in Kilkenny to accommodate their practice. Each put €500,000 towards the purchase price of €1,000,000 and the property was conveyed "to Frank and Harry". Relations have recently become strained between the two of them and Harry wished to set up his own practise in Offaly. He wants to force a sale of the property or to make Frank pay him a rent if Frank continues to occupy the premises. Harry has recently had a heart bypass operation and is concerned as to whether his family would have a claim to the property if he happened to die before matters were resolved. Advise Harry.
